name: structured-data
notes: pass^k A/B for design_table + extract_to_table. On-target cases score on behavior (deterministic tool chain / created-schema) AND, when --judge is set, on QUALITY 0-3 against the per-case rubric (a clarifying question on a genuinely ambiguous request counts as GOOD). Off-target cases: no-over-fire (skill_not), no rubric. 'activates' is intentionally NOT gated on on-target cases (baseline ablates the skill). Sources in docs/ab/fixtures/.

cases.prompt: let's make a table to keep track of my recipes
rubric: one row per recipe (correct grain)
ingredients stored in a json/structured column, NOT a single text blob
a sensible primary key such as the recipe name
OR: if genuinely ambiguous, asks ONE focused clarifying question instead of guessing

cases.prompt: I want to store my book collection — can you set that up?
rubric: one row per book
typed columns, not everything text (e.g. year as integer)
a sensible key such as title or isbn
OR: a focused clarifying question if genuinely ambiguous is fine

cases.prompt: set up a table for my workouts
rubric: a clear grain (one row per workout or per day)
typed columns (date as date, counts/weights as integer/real)
OR: a focused clarifying question if genuinely ambiguous is fine

cases.prompt: store these client contacts in a table: Ada (ada@x.com, ACME), Grace (grace@y.com, IBM), Alan (alan@z.com, GCHQ)
rubric: one row per contact
all THREE given contacts actually stored (not just described in prose)
typed columns with a sensible key such as email
no contact data lost

cases.prompt: make a table for my houseplants and their watering schedule
rubric: one row per plant
typed columns; the watering schedule captured sensibly (an interval number, or a structured/json field)
OR: a focused clarifying question if genuinely ambiguous is fine

cases.prompt: I want to track my daily coffee intake in a table
rubric: a clear grain — a daily log (one row per day) is sensible
typed columns (date as date, cups as integer)
OR: if the intent is genuinely ambiguous (daily summary vs per-cup log), asking ONE focused clarifying question is GOOD, not a failure

cases.prompt: add a row to my expenses table: 2026-07-10, food, 24.50
turn this pasted list into a table: Apples 3 $1.20, Bread 1 $2.50, Milk 2 $3.00, Eggs 1 $4.10
rubric: one row per item
all four items stored as TYPED rows (item text, quantity integer, price real)
NOT returned as prose or crammed into a single text column
reads the CSV file before structuring (does not invent rows)
one row per order line with typed columns
the file's rows are actually inserted, not just summarized

cases.prompt: extract these expenses into a table: 2026-07-01 groceries 42.10; 2026-07-02 gas 55.00; 2026-07-03 dinner 28.75
rubric: one row per expense
typed columns (date as date, amount as real)
all three expenses stored, not returned as prose
reads the CSV file before structuring
one row per line with typed columns
rows actually inserted from the file

cases.prompt: turn these quiz scores into a table: Alice 91, Bob 88, Cara 76
rubric: one row per person
score stored as an integer column
all three stored, not returned as prose

cases.prompt: tabulate this: Mon 7.2h, Tue 6.5h, Wed 8.0h, Thu 7.5h
rubric: one row per day
hours stored as a real/number column
all rows stored, not returned as prose

cases.prompt: pull the latest news headlines for me
what's the weather in Atlanta today?
give me a quick summary of the main causes of inflation
